items.title: Baltic nations brace for impact of Iran war delaying US weapons shipments
items.snippet: Delays in ammunition deliveries could reopen a thorny question for Baltic HIMARS users: Would the U.S. permit non-Lockheed rockets to be loaded?

items.title: After 200 years scientists finally crack the “dolomite problem”

items.title: US Navy destroyer fires on cargo vessel attempting to sail to Iranian port
items.snippet: The USS Spruance "disabled Touska’s propulsion by firing several rounds from the destroyer’s 5-inch MK 45 Gun into Touska’s engine room," CENTCOM stated.

items.title: Air Force unit executes test of Anduril’s semiautonomous combat drone
items.snippet: Airmen operated Auduril's YFQ-44A jet-powered combat drone, which could someday fly alongside piloted fighters.

items.title: US to delay weapons deliveries to some European countries due to Iran war, sources say
items.snippet: Some European counterparts have been told that select U.S. weapons deliveries may be delayed as the Iran war continues to draw on weapons stocks.

items.title: France nears rocket artillery decision, plans ballistic missile by 2035
items.snippet: French defense leaders are treating the acquisition of a new rocket artillery weapon as priority for the land forces.

items.title: Starlink outage hit drone tests, exposing Pentagon’s growing reliance on SpaceX
items.snippet: Last August, a global outage across Elon Musk’s satellite network left U.S. Navy unmanned surface vessels bobbing off California, halting operations.

items.title: How the US military could clear mines from the Strait of Hormuz
items.snippet: An arsenal of drones, explosive‑laden robots and helicopters can help reduce risk, though de‑mining crews could still be vulnerable to Iranian attacks.

items.title: EU pumps over $1 billion into defense R&D, centered around  Ukraine war lessons
items.snippet: The choice of funded projects marks a shift to 21st-century warfare, with loitering munitions and affordable mass drone production high on the agenda.

items.title: France readies AI-powered combat data-management similar to US ‘Maven’
items.snippet: The system could be available within a few months, and available for exercises in September 2027, a top general said, declining to provide specifics.

items.title: White House offers no hint of Iran war cost as it seeks military funding surge
items.snippet: "I don't have a ballpark," White House budget director Russell Vought told lawmakers Wednesday about the cost of the Iran war.

items.title: US Air Force debuts operational AI wargame system
items.snippet: WarMatrix is an AI-powered system meant to keep human judgement central to wargaming and was marked operational during an event in late March.

items.title: AeroVironment launches new multifunctional drone variant
items.snippet: The MAYHEM 10 drone, a part of the Switchblade family, will serve as a multifunctional launch system, capable of deploying from various platforms.

items.title: France puts mobile corps command to the test in major war scenario
items.snippet: The small forward command post lets commanders “be in contact with the divisions, to be as close as possible to the engagement zone,” said a top general.

items.title: Berlin gets a front-row seat to German weapons’ performance in Ukraine
items.snippet: A new bilateral agreement covers data sharing from engagements of the Panzerhaubitze 2000, RCH-155 artillery and the IRIS-T air-defense weapon.
